channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Shift in Nyanza politics as leaders embrace Ruto administration while Orengo faces backlash
item.description: Leaders from Nyanza region are increasingly engaging with President William Ruto’s administration, signalling a noticeable shift in the political landscape of the Luo‑Nyanza belt. Once seen as a stron...

item.title: Police investigate disappearance of former CS Raphael Tuju and his driver in Karen
item.description: Former Cabinet Secretary Raphael Tuju and his driver, Steve Mwanga, have been reported missing after their vehicle was found abandoned in the Karen area of Nairobi on Saturday evening. According to Tu...

item.title: Health PS Mary Muthoni Engages Murang’a Residents in Jamii Imara Mashinani Forum on Local Development Issues
item.description: Health Principal Secretary Mary Muthoni spearheaded the Jamii Imara Mashinani (JIM) citizen engagement forum in Gatanga Constituency, Murang’a County, on March 21, 2026. The event, held at Gatanga CCM...

item.title: Reviving Democracy: Local Government Accountability and the Fight for Human Rights
item.description: In commemorating the Sharpeville Massacre, Lesego Mahlangu calls for a renewed focus on local government accountability to honour the memory of those who died for dignity and equality. Despite the 199...

item.title: Police Officer Dies in Collision While Responding to Emergency Call
item.description: A police officer has tragically passed away after a serious crash while responding to an emergency call. PC Bradley Corke, 27, died on Saturday from critical injuries sustained in a collision on Thurs...

item.title: Iranian missile strike hits Dimona, raising fears about security near Israeli nuclear research facility
item.description: An Iranian ballistic missile struck the Israeli desert town of Dimona on Saturday evening, inflicting heavy damage on residential buildings and injuring dozens of people. The attack marked a major esc...

item.title: Daily Superquiz and Crossword Challenges for Subscribers
item.description: The article titled 'Superquiz, Monday, March 23' from The Age serves primarily as an announcement for readers interested in daily puzzle content available to subscribers. Authored by Michelle Stillman...

item.title: Orphans from Kibada enjoy Eid celebration at Fun City
item.description: More than 30 children from Busara Orphanage Centre in Kibada, Kigamboni, had a special Eid celebration at Fun City, Dar es Salaam. The event gave the youngsters a chance to enjoy the festive season ju...

item.title: Tanzania officially joins World Boxing, opening global doors for local athletes
item.description: The Boxing Federation of Tanzania (BFT) has proudly announced that Tanzania has been officially admitted as a member of World Boxing, the international body recognised by the International Olympic Com...

item.title: Tanzania and South Africa Enhance Partnership on Humanitarian Cooperation and Disaster Management
item.description: The Deputy Minister for Transport, David Kihenzile, has held a crucial meeting with senior leaders of the Southern African Red Cross Society in Johannesburg to enhance collaboration between Tanzania a...

item.title: Pope Leo condemns ongoing wars as a scandal for humanity
item.description: Pope Leo has expressed strong condemnation of the ongoing wars, particularly in the Middle East, calling them a 'scandal' for humanity. His comments reflect growing international frustration with the ...

item.title: Steve Nicol Criticizes Chelsea’s Performance Following Everton Loss
item.description: Former Liverpool defender Steve Nicol don come out sharply against Chelsea after dem waka lose 3-0 to Everton for Saturday match wey happen for Hill Dickinson Stadium. Nicol yan say the Chelsea player...

item.title: Living in the long middle: navigating life with chronic stage four cancer
item.description: The article offers a deeply reflective and personal essay from a psychologist living with stage four lung cancer, exploring what she calls 'the long middle' — a state between survival and imminent dea...

item.title: Lagos Task Force Faces Dissolution Threat Over Alleged Misconduct
item.description: The Lagos State Police Commissioner, Moshood Jimoh, don drop heavy warning to the Lagos Environmental and Special Offences Enforcement Unit, popularly known as Task Force. According to am, if the alle...

item.title: Archbishop Ndagoso calls for unity and peace among Nigerians during Eid celebration
item.description: Archbishop Matthew Man-Oso Ndagoso, wey be di Catholic Archbishop of Kaduna and di president of di Catholic Bishops' Conference of Nigeria (CBCN), don use him Eid al-Fitr message take show say Christi...

item.title: Two Weeks of Global Nuclear and Energy Developments to March 21, 2026
item.description: This article from nuclear-news compiles a wide range of global stories concerning nuclear energy, related environmental issues, and anti-nuclear activism during the two weeks leading up to March 21, 2...

item.title: Job scam traps 100 Kenyans in Myanmar as 39 are jailed
item.description: A job scam crisis has left over 100 Kenyans trapped in Myanmar, with 39 of them currently detained. The victims were lured by fake online job offers that promised high‑paying work, mainly in call cent...

item.title: Estonia names 24-man squad to face Kenya in FIFA Series as injured Arsenal goalkeeper misses out
item.description: Estonia have announced a 24-man squad to face Kenya’s Harambee Stars in the upcoming FIFA Series tournament to be held in Rwanda. The match is scheduled for March 27 at the Amahoro Stadium in Kigali. ...

item.title: 7 Healthy Drinks to Keep You Hydrated in Hot Weather
item.description: If water no dey your thing, no wahala. You fit still keep your body hydrated even when the sun dey shine hot with some drinks wey dey very common for Naija. Drinks like zobo, kunu, coconut water, fres...

item.title: Factional Battle Intensifies in Kunnathur Ahead of Kerala Assembly Elections 2026
item.description: The Kunnathur constituency in Kerala's Kollam district, a longstanding Left bastion, is once again witnessing a fierce contest between rival factions of the Revolutionary Socialist Party (RSP) as the ...
